circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit, designed by Hermann Tilke on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island, stretches 5. 281 kilometers and has served as the venue for Formula One's season finale since 2009. It represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One facility, following Bahrain's circuit and preceding those in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a street course that loops around a lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb and hosts the annual Formula One Australian Grand Prix alongside various support series. The venue holds the highest FIA safety classification for permanent circuits.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit was originally built as the Österreichring and served as the venue for the Austrian Grand Prix throughout the 1970s and 1980s. Following a redesign and renaming, the track resumed hosting the Austrian Grand Prix in the late 1990s and early 2000s after a decade away from the calendar.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street racing venue in Azerbaijan situated along Baku Boulevard, measuring 6. 003 km per lap and ranking as the fourth-longest track in Formula One. The circuit first hosted the 2016 European Grand Prix before becoming the permanent home of the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Inaugurated in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit is a 5. 412 km venue that hosted the Middle East's inaugural Formula One grand prix that year. Beyond Formula One, the track has accommodated various motorsport categories including GP2 racing, endurance competitions, and Australian V8 Supercars events, while maintaining FIA Grade 1 certification and offering multiple circuit configurations.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657-kilometer facility features a balanced mix of lengthy straightaways and diverse corner configurations that test multiple aspects of driver skill and vehicle performance. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstand facilities.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Situated in the Walloon region of Belgium near the town of Spa, this 7. 004 km circuit has hosted the Belgian Grand Prix continuously since 1985, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006. The venue boasts a storied history, having staged motor racing events since 1925 and serving as one of the original Formula One locations when the championship commenced in 1950.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve spans 4. 361 kilometers and serves as the permanent home of the Canadian Grand Prix on the Formula One calendar. Beyond its role as a premier single-seater venue, the track has hosted various international motorsport series including sports car championships and North American open-wheel and stock car competitions.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit, located in Shanghai's Jiading District, is a motorsport facility renowned for hosting the Chinese Grand Prix, which ran annually from 2004 to 2019 before resuming in 2024. The track has also begun hosting electric racing competitions since 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located near Silverstone in Northamptonshire, this English circuit has hosted the British Grand Prix since 1948 and notably staged the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After rotating with two rival venues between 1955 and 1986, the Grand Prix became a permanent fixture at this track from 1987 onwards, which additionally welcomes MotoGP competition.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring near Budapest hosted Formula One's first grand prix in the Eastern Bloc when it debuted in 1986, constructed in just eight months, making it the fastest-built F1 circuit at the time. Located in Mogyoród outside the capital, this 4. 381 km track was chosen over a proposed street circuit in Budapest's main park after government officials selected a permanent venue near a major highway instead.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian racing circuit constructed in 1922 near Milan, making it mainland Europe's oldest purpose-built track and only the third of its kind globally. Home to the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, the venue earns its "Temple of Speed" nickname from its extended straights and fast-paced corner design that encourage high-velocity racing.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807-kilometer motorsport venue situated in Mie Prefecture, Japan, and managed by Honda Mobilityland. The track, which accommodates 155,000 spectators, has become internationally recognized as a premier Formula One racing destination and host of the Japanese Super Formula championship.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that winds through Paradise, Nevada, incorporating famous landmarks along Las Vegas Boulevard such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas. The track traverses the heart of the entertainment district, weaving past the city's most iconic hotels and casinos that define the Las Vegas Strip.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: This 4. 3 km Mexico City circuit is named after two fatal racing brothers, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, who died in 1962 and 1971 respectively. The venue has hosted Formula One's Mexican Grand Prix in multiple eras, with the current iteration beginning in 2015 on its modern layout, distinct from the previous configuration used until 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary road course constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring a 3. 363-mile layout with 19 corners and qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph. Designed by specialist circuit architects, the track was built specifically to host the Miami Grand Prix, which joined the Formula One World Championship calendar in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a 3. 337 km street track that winds through Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts, circling the harbour in the Principality of Monaco. Though officially named after the circuit itself, it is widely known as Monte Carlo since the majority of its layout passes through that neighbourhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: The Zandvoort circuit is a 4. 259 km track situated in the Dutch dunes near the North Sea, approximately 35 km west of Amsterdam. After a lengthy absence, the venue rejoined Formula One in 2021 to host the Dutch Grand Prix, though this arrangement concludes in 2026.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit is a purpose-built motor racing facility situated north of Doha in Qatar, spanning 5. 419 kilometers with a notably long main straight extending over one kilometer. Holding FIA Grade 1 certification, the track features artificial grass lining designed to prevent sand intrusion in the desert environment.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Located in São Paulo, Brazil, Interlagos is a 4. 309 km counterclockwise circuit that opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula 1 driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aviation accident eight years earlier. The venue features a kart facility named after fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: A street circuit positioned along Jeddah's waterfront promenade bordering the Red Sea, this venue has hosted the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix annually since its inauguration in 2021 and began accommodating Formula E racing in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's Marina Bay area, incorporating sections of the Downtown Core and Kallang districts into its layout.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657 km Spanish circuit features a balanced combination of extended straights and varied corner configurations that test multiple driving skills. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 status and can accommodate approximately 140,700 spectators across its stands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas spans 3. 426 miles and serves as a premier FIA Grade 1 racing venue. The track hosts the United States Formula One Grand Prix alongside MotoGP and endurance racing events, while having previously accommodated various other motorsport series including IndyCar and sports car competitions.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a dual Thai-British driver currently racing for Williams in Formula One, competing under Thailand's flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso competes for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has claimed two world championships during his 23-season career and accumulated 32 Grand Prix victories. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans 24 Hours triumphs and the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to win both a Formula One title and the World Endurance drivers' championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli made his Formula One entry in 2025 and has already secured five race victories while competing for Mercedes.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British motorsport competitor who currently drives for Haas in the Formula One World Championship.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabi Bortoleto currently races in Formula One representing the Audi team. Known by his full name Gabriel Lourenzo Oliveira, he has established himself as a competitor in the sport's premier racing category.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Valtteri Bottas is a Finnish driver competing for Cadillac in Formula One, having achieved two runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship while with Mercedes in 2019 and 2020. Across his 13-season career, he has secured ten Grand Prix victories.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Argentine driver Franco Colapinto currently races for Alpine in the Formula One World Championship.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan combines endurance racing commitments in Europe's LMP2 category with a reserve role at Haas in Formula One, having made seven Grand Prix appearances during the 2024-2025 season.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The 2020 Italian Grand Prix victory with AlphaTauri remains a significant achievement in his career.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar represents France in Formula One while racing for Red Bull Racing, bringing both French and Algerian heritage to the sport.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari, holding an unprecedented seven world championship titles alongside Michael Schumacher. The veteran competitor maintains multiple all-time records including the highest number of race victories, pole positions, and podium appearances in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races for Audi in Formula One. Beyond his single-seater career, he has achieved significant success in endurance motorsport, notably winning the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand driver currently racing in Formula One for the Racing Bulls team.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc represents Monaco in Formula One competition, driving for Ferrari where he has secured nine race victories across his nine-season career. He came closest to claiming the world championship in 2022, finishing as runner-up in the drivers' standings.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver currently competing in the World Endurance Championship with BMW and the NASCAR Cup Series with Trackhouse Racing. He previously raced in Formula One across two separate stints between 2014 and 2024.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver for McLaren, claimed the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship title while competing for the team. Across his eight seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The versatile competitor achieved a significant milestone in his career by securing victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Mexican driver Sergio Pérez competes for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from over a decade and a half in the sport. He finished as championship runner-up in 2023 while driving for Red Bull and has claimed victory in six races throughout his career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri is an Australian driver competing for McLaren in Formula One, with nine Grand Prix victories accumulated over four racing seasons.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known by the nickname "the Honey Badger," competed in Formula One for 14 seasons between 2011 and 2024, securing eight race victories during his career in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell has claimed seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season Formula One career, currently racing for Mercedes.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, bringing experience from 12 seasons in the sport during which he has secured four Grand Prix victories.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver currently racing in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition, following stints in IMSA's LMP2 class and a two-year Formula One career spanning 2023 to 2024.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently racing for Aston Martin in the world championship.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver currently holding a reserve position within the Red Bull Racing organization, which includes Racing Bulls. He competed actively in Formula One as a regular competitor from 2021 through 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Dutch-Belgian driver Max Verstappen has secured four consecutive World Drivers' Championships between 2021 and 2024 while racing for Red Bull Racing. With 71 Grand Prix victories accumulated over his 12-season career, he remains a dominant force in Formula One under the Dutch flag.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu represents China as the sole Chinese driver to have competed in Formula One, having raced in the championship from 2022 through 2024. He currently holds a reserve driver position with Cadillac in the series.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team has competed as Alpine since 2021, when Renault rebranded its works outfit to promote its sports car division, maintaining this identity until Renault's Formula One departure at the end of 2025. Powered by Mercedes engines and racing under a French licence, Alpine's only victory came through Esteban Ocon's triumph at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has maintained a presence in Formula One through different organizational structures, with the current iteration operating as AMR GP Limited and competing under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari HP represents the racing operations of the Italian luxury automaker Ferrari in Formula One. As the sport's longest-standing and most accomplished team, Ferrari has participated continuously in every World Championship season since the series' inception in 1950, earning the nickname "the Prancing Horse" from their distinctive emblem.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas established this American-licensed Formula One team in 2014, with the squad entering the championship in 2016. The team has undergone leadership changes, with Ayao Komatsu taking over as team principal in 2024 following Guenther Steiner's departure after leading the outfit since its inception.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Swiss engineering firm Sauber Motorsport, established in 1970, achieved success in endurance racing including a Le Mans victory before debuting in Formula One in 1993. Following decades of competition as an independent constructor, the team concluded its F1 operations in 2025 as the sport's fourth-longest-serving constructor, subsequently becoming part of Audi's planned factory team programme following its acquisition in 2024.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren is a British Formula One outfit headquartered in Woking that competes under corporate sponsorship arrangements. The team operates as part of the wider McLaren Group, with ownership ultimately held by Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has competed in Formula One continuously since 1954, both as a constructor and engine supplier. The team, currently based in Brackley and operating under a German licence, became jointly owned by Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and team principal Toto Wolff from January 2022. Mercedes teams are traditionally known as the "Silver Arrows".

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in 2024 as part of Red Bull's two-team operation. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility near Milton Keynes, the squad functions as a sister team to Red Bull Racing under Austrian ownership.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that commenced operations in 2024 as Red Bull GmbH's second racing outfit, alongside Red Bull Racing. Headquartered in Faenza with an additional facility near Milton Keynes, the team represents the energy drink conglomerate's expanded presence in the sport.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, competing under an Austrian licence from their English base, emerged as a Formula One competitor in 2005 and has become one of two top-tier teams within the Red Bull GmbH conglomerate. Christian Horner led the organization from its inception through 2024, with Laurent Mekies assuming the team principal role in 2025.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering stands as a British Formula One team founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, emerging from the ashes of Williams's previous failed racing venture. Operating from its Grove, Oxfordshire headquarters, the constructor competes in the 2026 season as Atlassian Williams F1 Team.
